$comment: c23 (D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR): comma-separated declarators sharing one head base type in a struct body -- `struct P{int *a,b;}` (the `*` binds to a only; b is int) and `struct Q{int x,y[2],*z;}`. Each field is written then read back through a non-inlined call so it round-trips through its own slot at its own byte offset. A per-slot suffix LEAK (b->int*, or y[2]/z* leaking onto a neighbor) shifts an offset and the readback drifts off 42. Release arm (optimizedPipelines) so the layout/codegen path is exercised end-to-end across all targets.
